text:
Once upon a quaint seaside town named Twiddlebum, there lived a peculiar man named Bertie Burrows. Bertie was notorious for never speaking about himself. Everyone had their theories. Maybe he didn't own a chair.

One crisp autumn day, Bertie found himself wandering along the tide pools behind Little Cove Beach. He'd always been fascinated by the sea—a deep silence would enthrall him there. Today however, something was whispering.

"What are you staring at?" A small voice snapped.

Startled, Bertie whipped his head around. Nothing. Just his reflection on the shore. "Didn't mean to scare ya."

But shadows had gathered along the shore. They watched him blink.

"The sea whispers." Bertie muttered softly.

Just then, a jellyfish drifted past. Bertie didn't flinch. He just listened closer.

"You're dreaming."

Dreaming? Bertie chuckled.

A starfish glided closer. Bertie tilted his head. There was an innocence about
